perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is twenty-four (24) months. The Agreement may be renewed, subject to mutual written approval. Notwithstanding the completion, expiration or termination of this Agreement, the indemnities warranties and undertakings contained or referred to in this Agreement shall continue to subsist for as long as may be necessary for the purpose of giving effect to each one and every one of them.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is twenty-four (24) months, unless earlier terminated by either party with thirty (30) days written notice. The Agreement may be renewed, subject to mutual written approval. Notwithstanding the completion, expiration or termination of this Agreement, the indemnities warranties and undertakings contained or referred to in this Agreement shall continue to subsist for a period of 12 months after termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the duration of indemnities, warranties, and undertakings. Originally, these were meant to last as long as necessary to give effect to each one. Now, they are limited to 12 months after termination, potentially conflicting with obligations that inherently require a longer duration. This contradicts the original intent to give effect to each one of them, where some of them might need longer than 12 months to take effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twenty-four (24) months, unless earlier terminated by either party with thirty (30) days written notice. The Agreement may be renewed, subject to mutual written approval. Notwithstanding the completion, expiration or termination of this Agreement, the indemnities warranties and undertakings contained or referred to in this Agreement shall continue to subsist for a period of 12 months after termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that indemnities, warranties, and undertakings continue to subsist for as long as necessary. The changed text limits this duration to 12 months after termination. This directly contradicts the original intent, creating an in-text contradiction because some indemnities, warranties, and undertakings may inherently require more than 12 months to take effect, rendering the original agreement unenforceable if those protections are cut short. The 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition applies, as obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: TKCI affirms that it shall not have any claim towards SIC if the management decides not to sign the agreement in the investigation stage, and before any agreements are signed, for any reasons whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: TKCI affirms that it shall not have any claim towards SIC if the management decides not to sign the agreement in the investigation stage, and before any agreements are signed, for any reasons whatsoever, unless TKCI has expended significant resources in the preliminary investigation at the explicit written request of SIC. In such cases, TKCI will be entitled to reimbursement of documented expenses.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that TKCI has no claim towards SIC if the agreement isn't signed. The modified text introduces an exception: if TKCI expended significant resources at SIC's written request, they are entitled to reimbursement. This creates a contradiction, as one part says 'no claim for any reason whatsoever' while the other allows a claim under specific conditions. This directly contradicts the earlier statement of 'any reasons whatsoever'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TKCI affirms that it shall not have any claim towards SIC if the management decides not to sign the agreement in the investigation stage, and before any agreements are signed, for any reasons whatsoever, unless TKCI has expended significant resources in the preliminary investigation at the explicit written request of SIC. In such cases, TKCI will be entitled to reimbursement of documented expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains an absolute statement: "TKCI affirms that it shall not have any claim towards SIC...for any reasons whatsoever." The changed text introduces an exception to this absolute statement: "unless TKCI has expended significant resources..." This exception directly contradicts the 'for any reasons whatsoever' clause, creating a clear and significant in-text contradiction as defined by the "Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction" perturbation definition. The 'Contradicted Text' reinforces this by restating the original absolute statement. This alters the agreement's understanding of when TKCI has a claim, which creates a contradiction within the text.

perturbation.original_text: Net revenue from business operations created by Holding Company for the alliance will be distributed by Holding Company equally - 50/50 - to TKCI and SIC:
perturbation.changed_text: Net revenue from business operations created by Holding Company for the alliance will be distributed by Holding Company with 60% to TKCI and 40% to SIC until TKCI recoups its initial investment, after which the distribution will revert to 50/50.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines a 50/50 split of net revenue. The modified text introduces a conditional distribution of 60/40 in favor of TKCI until the initial investment is recouped, creating a contradiction. This contradicts the statement of net revenue being 50/50, because only after TKCI's recovery of investment is the 50/50 share being applied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Net revenue from business operations created by Holding Company for the alliance will be distributed by Holding Company with 60% to TKCI and 40% to SIC until TKCI recoups its initial investment, after which the distribution will revert to 50/50:
perturbation.justification: The original text states a 50/50 revenue split. The changed text introduces a 60/40 split until TKCI recoups its initial investment, after which it reverts to 50/50. This creates a direct contradiction, as the initial revenue distribution is no longer consistently 50/50, violating the original agreement. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text and states a conditional circumstance for a 60/40 split. This is an example of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'. This creates an obvious and impactful conflict within the document.
